input.content:
---
title: "cosmo-smoke v0.7 — Monorepo, WebSocket, gRPC Build Tag"
created: 2026-04-18
status: approved
roadmap:
  - ROAD-010
  - ROAD-031
  - ROAD-030
---

# cosmo-smoke v0.7 — Monorepo, WebSocket, gRPC Build Tag

## Why

v0.6 expanded assertion coverage with 4 new connectivity checks. v0.7 addresses three gaps:

1. **Monorepo support (ROAD-010)** — CosmoLabs has ~95 projects. Many live in monorepos. Currently users must `cd` into each subdirectory or write a wrapper script. Auto-discovery makes `smoke run --monorepo` work from the repo root.

2. **WebSocket assertion (ROAD-031)** — Real-time apps use WebSocket extensively. A connect-send-expect assertion fills the gap between HTTP checks and custom shell scripts.

3. **Optional gRPC module (ROAD-030)** — The gRPC health check adds 121MB of dependencies and inflates the binary from ~8MB to 13.9MB. Most users don't need gRPC. An opt-in build tag lets them skip it.

## Design Decisions

1. **Monorepo is explicit opt-in.** `--monorepo` flag or `monorepo: true` in root config. No auto-detection — avoids accidental scans of large non-monorepo directories.
2. **Unlimited discovery depth.** Real monorepos have varied layouts (`services/team-a/api/`). Depth limits would be arbitrary.
3. **WebSocket via stdlib only.** The connect-send-expect pattern only needs HTTP upgrade + frame parsing. No external WS library. ~80 lines.
4. **gRPC is opt-in via `-tags grpc`.** Default build excludes gRPC. Users who need it add the tag. No behavior change for existing users.
5. **No new external dependencies.** All three features use stdlib only. The "minimal deps" principle is maintained.

## Feature 1: Monorepo Sub-Config Discovery

### Behavior

- `smoke run --monorepo` triggers discovery from cwd (or config file dir)
- `monorepo: true` in root `.smoke.yaml` settings also enables it
- CLI flag takes precedence over config
- Walks all subdirectories (unlimited depth), finds `.smoke.yaml` files
- Skips: `.git/`, `node_modules/`, `vendor/`, `__pycache__/`, `dist/`, `build/`, `target/`
- Additional skip dirs configurable via `monorepo_exclude` in settings
- Each discovered config runs as a sub-suite with its own project name
- Reporter shows per-project results + aggregate summary
- Exit code 1 if any sub-suite has failures
- `--tag`/`--exclude-tag`/`--fail-fast` apply across all sub-suites
- `--dry-run` lists all discovered configs without running
- Root `.smoke.yaml` is optional — can use `--monorepo` flag alone

### Schema Changes

```yaml
# Root .smoke.yaml (optional)
version: 1
project: my-monorepo
settings:
  monorepo: true
  monorepo_exclude:
    - "internal/"
    - "examples/"
```

New fields on `Settings`:
```go
Monorepo         bool     `yaml:"monorepo,omitempty"`
MonorepoExclude  []string `yaml:"monorepo_exclude,omitempty"`
```

### Implementation

New `internal/monorepo/` package:
- `Discover(root string, exclude []string) ([]SubConfig, error)` — walks directory tree
- `SubConfig` struct: `{Path, Project string}`
- Skips common non-project dirs by default

Runner changes:
- New `RunMonorepo(opts RunOptions) (*SuiteResult, error)` method
- Runs each sub-config as its own `Runner` instance
- Aggregates results into a single `SuiteResult`
- Reporter shows project-level grouping

CLI changes:
- `--monorepo` flag on `smoke run`
- `run.go`: check flag + config, call `RunMonorepo` when active

### Output Example

```
  ● api: Compiles               ✓
  ● api: Tests pass              ✓
  ● api: Health endpoint         ✓
    api: 3 passed  (2.1s)

  ● worker: Builds              ✓
  ● worker: Connects to Redis    ✓
    worker: 2 passed  (1.8s)

  ● auth: Builds                ✓
  ● auth: JWT validation         ✓
    auth: 2 passed  (1.2s)

  Total: 3 projects, 7 passed  (5.1s)
```

### Testing

| Test | Description |
|------|-------------|
| TestDiscover_FindsSubConfigs | Create temp dir with 2 subdirs each having `.smoke.yaml`, verify both found |
| TestDiscover_SkipsIgnoredDirs | `node_modules/` with `.smoke.yaml` is skipped |
| TestDiscover_CustomExclude | `monorepo_exclude` dirs are skipped |
| TestDiscover_DeepNesting | Finds config 3+ levels deep |
| TestRunMonorepo_AllPass | Run monorepo suite, all pass |
| TestRunMonorepo_PartialFail | One sub-suite fails, aggregate exit code is 1 |
| TestRunMonorepo_TagFilter | `--tag build` runs only tagged tests across all sub-suites |
| TestRunMonorepo_DryRun | Lists discovered configs without running |

## Feature 2: WebSocket Assertion

### Schema

```yaml
expect:
  websocket:
    url: "ws://localhost:8080/ws"      # ws:// or wss:// (required)
    send: '{"type": "ping"}'           # optional message to send after connect
    expect_contains: "pong"            # substring match on received message
    expect_matches: "connected.*true"  # regex match (alternative to contains)
    timeout: 5s                        # connection + response timeout
```

New struct:
```go
type WebSocketCheck struct {
    URL            string   `yaml:"url"`
    Send           string   `yaml:"send,omitempty"`
    ExpectContains string   `yaml:"expect_contains,omitempty"`
    ExpectMatches  string   `yaml:"expect_matches,omitempty"`
    Timeout        Duration `yaml:"timeout,omitempty"`
}
```

New field on `Expect`:
```go
WebSocket *WebSocketCheck `yaml:"websocket,omitempty"`
```

### Validation

- `url` is required, must start with `ws://` or `wss://`
- At least one of `expect_contains` or `expect_matches` is required
- If `expect_matches` is set, must compile as valid Go regex

### Implementation

Pure stdlib WebSocket client (~80 lines):
- HTTP GET with `Upgrade: websocket`, `Connection: Upgrade` headers
- `Sec-WebSocket-Key` via `crypto/rand` + `base64`
- `Sec-WebSocket-Accept` verification via `crypto/sha1`
- Frame parsing: opcode 1 (text), opcode 8 (close), opcode 9 (ping)
- Masking for client-to-server frames (required by RFC 6455)

New `CheckWebSocket` function in `internal/runner/assertion.go` or new `assertion_ws.go`.

Error handling:
- Connection refused → "connection failed: ..."
- Timeout → "timed out after Xs waiting for message"
- Close frame → "server closed connection: <reason>"
- No match → "received <message> did not contain/match <pattern>"

### Testing

| Test | Description |
|------|-------------|
| TestCheckWebSocket_ExpectContains_Pass | Server echoes, send "ping", expect "ping" |
| TestCheckWebSocket_ExpectMatches_Pass | Server sends JSON, regex matches |
| TestCheckWebSocket_NoMatch_Fail | Server sends "hello", expect "pong" |
| TestCheckWebSocket_ConnectionRefused | Connect to unused port, verify graceful failure |

## Feature 3: Optional gRPC Module via Build Tag

### Approach

Opt-in: `-tags grpc`. Default build excludes gRPC deps entirely.

### File Changes

```
internal/runner/
  assertion_grpc.go          # //go:build grpc — current gRPC code, moved from assertion.go
  assertion_grpc_stub.go     # //go:build !grpc — stub returning "not available"
```

`assertion_grpc.go` contains:
- `CheckGRPCHealth` function (moved from `assertion.go`)
- gRPC-related imports (`google.golang.org/grpc`, healthpb, etc.)

`assertion_grpc_stub.go` contains:
```go
//go:build !grpc

func CheckGRPCHealth(check *schema.GRPCHealthCheck) AssertionResult {
    return AssertionResult{
        Type:     "grpc_health",
        Expected: check.Address,
        Actual:   "grpc_health not available — rebuild with -tags grpc",
        Passed:   false,
    }
}
```

### Impact

| Build | Command | Binary Size | gRPC Available |
|-------|---------|-------------|----------------|
| Default | `go build -o smoke .` | ~8MB | No (stub returns error) |
| With gRPC | `go build -tags grpc -o smoke .` | ~13.9MB | Yes |

- `go.mod` unchanged — build tags only control compilation
- Schema struct `GRPCHealthCheck` remains in schema.go (always parsed, validation always works)
- Runner wiring in `runner.go` unchanged — calls `CheckGRPCHealth` regardless
- No behavior change for users who install via `go install` (they get default build)

### Testing

Existing gRPC tests move to `assertion_grpc_test.go` with `//go:build grpc` tag. New stub test:
```go
//go:build !grpc

func TestCheckGRPCHealth_StubReturns(t *testing.T) {
    result := CheckGRPCHealth(&schema.GRPCHealthCheck{Address: "localhost:9090"})
    if result.Passed {
        t.Error("stub should not pass")
    }
    if !strings.Contains(result.Actual, "grpc") {
        t.Error("should mention grpc in output")
    }
}
```

## File Scope

```yaml
files_modified:
  - cmd/run.go                      # --monorepo flag
  - internal/schema/schema.go       # new Settings fields, WebSocketCheck struct
  - internal/schema/validate.go     # WebSocket + monorepo validation
  - internal/runner/runner.go       # RunMonorepo method, WebSocket wiring
  - internal/runner/assertion.go    # Remove gRPC code, add CheckWebSocket
  - CLAUDE.md                       # Update assertion table
files_created:
  - internal/monorepo/discover.go   # SubConfig discovery
  - internal/monorepo/discover_test.go
  - internal/runner/assertion_grpc.go       # +build grpc
  - internal/runner/assertion_grpc_stub.go  # +build !grpc
  - internal/runner/assertion_ws_test.go    # WebSocket tests
  - internal/runner/assertion_grpc_test.go  # gRPC tests (moved, +build grpc)
```

## v0.7 Release Scope

Headline: "cosmo-smoke v0.7 — Monorepo, WebSocket, Lean Binary"

- Monorepo sub-config auto-discovery (ROAD-010)
- WebSocket connect-send-expect assertion (ROAD-031)
- Optional gRPC module via build tag (ROAD-030)
- ~258+ tests passing (246 current + ~12 new)

- `Sec-WebSocket-Key` via `crypto/rand` + `base64`
- `Sec-WebSocket-Accept` verification via `crypto/sha1`
- Frame parsing: opcode 1 (text), opcode 8 (close), opcode 9 (ping)
- Masking for client-to-server frames (required by RFC 6455)

New `CheckWebSocket` function in `internal/runner/assertion.go` or new `assertion_ws.go`.

Error handling:
- Connection refused → "connection failed: ..."
- Timeout → "timed out after Xs waiting for message"
- Close frame → "server closed connection: <reason>"
- No match → "received <message> did not contain/match <pattern>"

### Testing

| Test | Description |
|------|-------------|
| TestCheckWebSocket_ExpectContains_Pass | Server echoes, send "ping", expect "ping" |
| TestCheckWebSocket_ExpectMatches_Pass | Server sends JSON, regex matches |
| TestCheckWebSocket_NoMatch_Fail | Server sends "hello", expect "pong" |
| TestCheckWebSocket_ConnectionRefused | Connect to unused port, verify graceful failure |

## Feature 3: Optional gRPC Module via Build Tag

### Approach

Opt-in: `-tags grpc`. Default build excludes gRPC deps entirely.

### File Changes

```
internal/runner/
  assertion_grpc.go          # //go:build grpc — current gRPC code, moved from assertion.go
  assertion_grpc_stub.go     # //go:build !grpc — stub returning "not available"
```

`assertion_grpc.go` contains:
- `CheckGRPCHealth` function (moved from `assertion.go`)
- gRPC-related imports (`google.golang.org/grpc`, healthpb, etc.)

`assertion_grpc_stub.go` contains:
```go
//go:build !grpc

func CheckGRPCHealth(check *schema.GRPCHealthCheck) AssertionResult {
    return AssertionResult{
        Type:     "grpc_health",
        Expected: check.Address,
        Actual:   "grpc_health not available — rebuild with -tags grpc",
        Passed:   false,
    }
}
```

### Impact

| Build | Command | Binary Size | gRPC Available |
|-------|---------|-------------|----------------|
| Default | `go build -o smoke .` | ~8MB | No (stub returns error) |
| With gRPC | `go build -tags grpc -o smoke .` | ~13.9MB | Yes |

- `go.mod` unchanged — build tags only control compilation
- Schema struct `GRPCHealthCheck` remains in schema.go (always parsed, validation always works)
- Runner wiring in `runner.go` unchanged — calls `CheckGRPCHealth` regardless
- No behavior change for users who install via `go install` (they get default build)

### Testing

Existing gRPC tests move to `assertion_grpc_test.go` with `//go:build grpc` tag. New stub test:
```go
//go:build !grpc

func TestCheckGRPCHealth_StubReturns(t *testing.T) {
    result := CheckGRPCHealth(&schema.GRPCHealthCheck{Address: "localhost:9090"})
    if result.Passed {
        t.Error("stub should not pass")
    }
    if !strings.Contains(result.Actual, "grpc") {
        t.Error("should mention grpc in output")
    }
}
```

## File Scope

```yaml
files_modified:
  - cmd/run.go                      # --monorepo flag
  - internal/schema/schema.go       # new Settings fields, WebSocketCheck struct
  - internal/schema/validate.go     # WebSocket + monorepo validation
  - internal/runner/runner.go       # RunMonorepo method, WebSocket wiring
  - internal/runner/assertion.go    # Remove gRPC code, add CheckWebSocket
  - CLAUDE.md                       # Update assertion table
files_created:
  - internal/monorepo/discover.go   # SubConfig discovery
  - internal/monorepo/discover_test.go
  - internal/runner/assertion_grpc.go       # +build grpc
  - internal/runner/assertion_grpc_stub.go  # +build !grpc
  - internal/runner/assertion_ws_test.go    # WebSocket tests
  - internal/runner/assertion_grpc_test.go  # gRPC tests (moved, +build grpc)
```

## v0.7 Release Scope

Headline: "cosmo-smoke v0.7 — Monorepo, WebSocket, Lean Binary"

- Monorepo sub-config auto-discovery (ROAD-010)
- WebSocket connect-send-expect assertion (ROAD-031)
- Optional gRPC module via build tag (ROAD-030)
- ~258+ tests passing (246 current + ~12 new)

# /please-continue - Resume Interrupted Workflow

Signal that you're done with your interruption and Claude should resume exactly where it left off.

## Usage

```bash
/please-continue
```

No arguments. No flags. Just resume.

## Instructions

When invoked:

1. **Check for unmerged agent work** — run `git worktree list` and check for agent worktrees from this session that haven't been merged. If found, merge them first via `ccs merge`.
2. **Check uncommitted work** — `git status --short` for anything that needs committing
3. **Check TaskList** — if tasks exist, find the current in_progress task
4. **Review conversation history** — identify what you were doing before the interruption
5. **Acknowledge the interruption** — briefly confirm any new context the user provided
6. **Resume the workflow** — pick up at the exact step you were on

### Rules

- Do NOT restart the task from scratch
- Do NOT re-read files already in context
- Do NOT re-run commands already executed
- If the user's interruption changes the plan, adapt — don't ignore it
- Always check for background agents that may have completed while the user was talking

### Output

```
Resuming: [brief description of what you were doing]
[If unmerged agents]: Merging N agent worktrees first...
[If user added context]: Noted: [1-line acknowledgment]
```

Then continue working silently — no ceremony needed.

## Related

`/remind-me` * `/whatsup` * `/recall`

Context: cosmo-smoke is a Go smoke test runner. v0.7 adds monorepo sub-config discovery, WebSocket assertion, and optional gRPC build tag. Current: 24 assertion types, Cobra + Lipgloss + yaml.v3 + gjson.

Report: Issues (MUST fix), Suggestions (nice-to-have), Verdict (APPROVED or ISSUES_FOUND).

content.text:
I now have a thorough understanding of the codebase and the spec. Here is the review.

---

## Spec Review: v0.7 Monorepo, WebSocket, gRPC Build Tag

### Issues (MUST fix before implementation)

**ISSUE-1: WebSocket `Timeout` type is wrong in the spec struct**

The spec defines `Timeout Duration` using a custom `Duration` type. But the custom `Duration` type in `internal/schema/schema.go` (line 205) is `schema.Duration`, not a bare `Duration`. The struct definition should use `schema.Duration` (or just `Duration` since it is in the same package -- this is fine in the schema package but the spec should be explicit about this being the existing custom type, not `time.Duration`). Minor naming clarity, but more importantly: the YAML example shows `timeout: 5s` which works, so no functional issue there. However, the spec does not state a default timeout. Every other check that takes a timeout (HTTPCheck, GRPCHealthCheck, URLReachableCheck) has a documented or code-level default. The WebSocket check should state its default (e.g., "defaults to 10s" or similar).

**ISSUE-2: gRPC stub function signature is wrong**

The spec shows the stub as:
```go
func CheckGRPCHealth(check *schema.GRPCHealthCheck) AssertionResult {
```

But looking at the runner wiring in `runner.go` line 387-392, the runner calls `CheckGRPCHealth` and appends a single `AssertionResult`. This is correct. However, the stub file uses `//go:build !grpc` and the real file uses `//go:build grpc`. Both files are in `internal/runner/`, and `AssertionResult` is defined in `assertion.go` in the same package -- this is fine. But the spec says the stub returns `AssertionResult` directly while the real function also returns `AssertionResult` directly (not a slice). This is consistent. 

The real issue: the stub imports `schema` package for `schema.GRPCHealthCheck`. The spec does not mention that the stub file needs the `schema` import. More critically, the spec does not address what happens at **validation time**. Currently `Validate()` in `validate.go` does not validate gRPC-specific fields. This is fine, but the spec says "Schema struct `GRPCHealthCheck` remains in schema.go (always parsed, validation always works)" -- this is accurate. No issue here on second analysis.

**ISSUE-3: Monorepo test count is wrong (baseline claim)**

The spec claims "~246 current + ~12 new = ~258+ tests". The actual test count from the codebase is 164 test functions across 9 test files (as counted by `func Test` occurrences). The "246" figure is incorrect. This is a cosmetic issue in the spec but should be corrected to avoid confusion during implementation.

**ISSUE-4: WebSocket validation rules are incomplete**

The spec says "at least one of `expect_contains` or `expect_matches` is required." But what if neither is provided? What if `send` is provided without either expectation? Is the test just a "connect succeeds" check? The spec should clarify: if neither `expect_contains` nor `expect_matches` is set, is the assertion a pure "connection succeeded" check, or is it a validation error?

This matters because for some WebSocket tests, just confirming the connection opens and does not close immediately is a valid smoke test.

**ISSUE-5: WebSocket `send` is optional but no behavior without `send` is specified**

If `send` is empty and only `expect_contains`/`expect_matches` is set, does the check wait for the server to send an unsolicited message? What is the read behavior? The spec says "connect-send-expect" but the schema makes `send` optional, which creates two distinct flows: (1) connect-send-expect, (2) connect-and-wait. Both need to be documented.

**ISSUE-6: `assertion_ws_test.go` tests require a WebSocket test server**

The test table lists tests like `TestCheckWebSocket_ExpectContains_Pass` which require a running WebSocket server. The spec does not specify how the test server is provisioned. Unlike the gRPC tests which use `grpc.NewServer()` from the test code, a stdlib WebSocket implementation needs a test HTTP server that upgrades to WebSocket. The spec should specify using `httptest.NewServer` with a custom handler that does the upgrade handshake using the same stdlib frame logic. This is feasible but needs to be documented or the test plan is incomplete.

**ISSUE-7: Monorepo feature missing `--watch` interaction**

The spec says `--tag`/`--exclude-tag`/`--fail-fast`/`--dry-run` apply across all sub-suites. But it does not mention `--watch`. Does `--monorepo --watch` re-discover sub-configs on each re-run? Does it watch all subdirectories for changes? The `--watch` flag already exists in `cmd/run.go` and this interaction must be addressed.

### Suggestions (nice-to-have, non-blocking)

**SUG-1: Monorepo should report which sub-configs failed by name in the exit summary**

The output example shows per-project grouping, but the spec does not show what the final summary line looks like when there are failures. A clear "FAILED: api (1), worker (0)" style summary at the end would help CI readability.

**SUG-2: WebSocket should support `expect_json_path` for structured message matching**

WebSocket servers often send JSON. A `json_field`-style assertion on received messages would be more useful than raw substring/regex. This could be a v0.8 enhancement.

**SUG-3: Monorepo `monorepo_exclude` patterns should support globbing**

The spec shows `monorepo_exclude: ["internal/", "examples/"]` as literal prefixes. Supporting glob patterns like `"*_test/"` or `"tmp-*"` would be more flexible. Not critical for v0.7.

**SUG-4: The gRPC build tag approach could use a `//go:build ignore` file to fail at go.mod level**

Currently, `go.mod` still lists the gRPC dependencies even though the default build does not use them. Users who `go get` the module still pull all gRPC transitive deps. If the goal is a lean dependency graph, a separate module or `replace` directive might be needed. The spec acknowledges "go.mod unchanged" but does not discuss this tradeoff.

**SUG-5: WebSocket binary frames are not addressed**

The frame parsing section mentions opcode 1 (text), 8 (close), 9 (ping). Binary frames (opcode 2) are common in production WebSocket apps. If a server sends binary frames, the check should either handle them or document that only text frames are supported.

**SUG-6: Add `TestDiscover_NoSmokeFiles` test case**

The monorepo test table has no test for "directory tree exists but contains zero `.smoke.yaml` files." This should return an empty slice and the runner should handle it gracefully (possibly exit with an error like "no smoke configs found").

### Verdict: **ISSUES_FOUND**

Seven issues must be addressed before this spec is ready for implementation. The most impactful are:

1. **ISSUE-4/ISSUE-5**: WebSocket "send is optional" creates an undocumented connect-without-send flow that changes the assertion semantics entirely.
2. **ISSUE-7**: `--watch` + `--monorepo` interaction is undefined and will cause ambiguous implementation.
3. **ISSUE-6**: Test infrastructure for WebSocket is not specified; the tests cannot be written without knowing how the test server works.

The remaining issues (test count baseline, default timeout) are lower severity but should still be corrected for accuracy. The spec is otherwise well-structured: schema changes are clear, the gRPC build tag approach is sound, the monorepo design is pragmatic, and the file scope is accurate.

The monorepo test table has no test for "directory tree exists but contains zero `.smoke.yaml` files." This should return an empty slice and the runner should handle it gracefully (possibly exit with an error like "no smoke configs found").

### Verdict: **ISSUES_FOUND**

Seven issues must be addressed before this spec is ready for implementation. The most impactful are:

1. **ISSUE-4/ISSUE-5**: WebSocket "send is optional" creates an undocumented connect-without-send flow that changes the assertion semantics entirely.
2. **ISSUE-7**: `--watch` + `--monorepo` interaction is undefined and will cause ambiguous implementation.
3. **ISSUE-6**: Test infrastructure for WebSocket is not specified; the tests cannot be written without knowing how the test server works.

The remaining issues (test count baseline, default timeout) are lower severity but should still be corrected for accuracy. The spec is otherwise well-structured: schema changes are clear, the gRPC build tag approach is sound, the monorepo design is pragmatic, and the file scope is accurate.

content.text: Good review. Let me fix the issues in the spec.

content.content:
1	---
2	title: "cosmo-smoke v0.7 — Monorepo, WebSocket, gRPC Build Tag"
3	created: 2026-04-18
4	status: approved
5	roadmap:
6	  - ROAD-010
7	  - ROAD-031
8	  - ROAD-030
9	---
10	
11	# cosmo-smoke v0.7 — Monorepo, WebSocket, gRPC Build Tag
12	
13	## Why
14	
15	v0.6 expanded assertion coverage with 4 new connectivity checks. v0.7 addresses three gaps:
16	
17	1. **Monorepo support (ROAD-010)** — CosmoLabs has ~95 projects. Many live in monorepos. Currently users must `cd` into each subdirectory or write a wrapper script. Auto-discovery makes `smoke run --monorepo` work from the repo root.
18	
19	2. **WebSocket assertion (ROAD-031)** — Real-time apps use WebSocket extensively. A connect-send-expect assertion fills the gap between HTTP checks and custom shell scripts.
20	
21	3. **Optional gRPC module (ROAD-030)** — The gRPC health check adds 121MB of dependencies and inflates the binary from ~8MB to 13.9MB. Most users don't need gRPC. An opt-in build tag lets them skip it.
22	
23	## Design Decisions
24	
25	1. **Monorepo is explicit opt-in.** `--monorepo` flag or `monorepo: true` in root config. No auto-detection — avoids accidental scans of large non-monorepo directories.
26	2. **Unlimited discovery depth.** Real monorepos have varied layouts (`services/team-a/api/`). Depth limits would be arbitrary.
27	3. **WebSocket via stdlib only.** The connect-send-expect pattern only needs HTTP upgrade + frame parsing. No external WS library. ~80 lines.
28	4. **gRPC is opt-in via `-tags grpc`.** Default build excludes gRPC. Users who need it add the tag. No behavior change for existing users.
29	5. **No new external dependencies.** All three features use stdlib only. The "minimal deps" principle is maintained.
30	
31	## Feature 1: Monorepo Sub-Config Discovery
32	
33	### Behavior
34	
35	- `smoke run --monorepo` triggers discovery from cwd (or config file dir)
36	- `monorepo: true` in root `.smoke.yaml` settings also enables it
37	- CLI flag takes precedence over config
38	- Walks all subdirectories (unlimited depth), finds `.smoke.yaml` files
39	- Skips: `.git/`, `node_modules/`, `vendor/`, `__pycache__/`, `dist/`, `build/`, `target/`
40	- Additional skip dirs configurable via `monorepo_exclude` in settings
41	- Each discovered config runs as a sub-suite with its own project name
42	- Reporter shows per-project results + aggregate summary
43	- Exit code 1 if any sub-suite has failures
44	- `--tag`/`--exclude-tag`/`--fail-fast` apply across all sub-suites
45	- `--dry-run` lists all discovered configs without running
46	- Root `.smoke.yaml` is optional — can use `--monorepo` flag alone
47	
48	### Schema Changes
49	
50	```yaml
51	# Root .smoke.yaml (optional)
52	version: 1
53	project: my-monorepo
54	settings:
55	  monorepo: true
56	  monorepo_exclude:
57	    - "internal/"
58	    - "examples/"
59	```
60	
61	New fields on `Settings`:
62	```go
63	Monorepo         bool     `yaml:"monorepo,omitempty"`
64	MonorepoExclude  []string `yaml:"monorepo_exclude,omitempty"`
65	```
66	
67	### Implementation
68	
69	New `internal/monorepo/` package:
70	- `Discover(root string, exclude []string) ([]SubConfig, error)` — walks directory tree
71	- `SubConfig` struct: `{Path, Project string}`
72	- Skips common non-project dirs by default
73	
74	Runner changes:
75	- New `RunMonorepo(opts RunOptions) (*SuiteResult, error)` method
76	- Runs each sub-config as its own `Runner` instance
77	- Aggregates results into a single `SuiteResult`
78	- Reporter shows project-level grouping
79	
80	CLI changes:
81	- `--monorepo` flag on `smoke run`
82	- `run.go`: check flag + config, call `RunMonorepo` when active
83	
84	### Output Example
85	
86	```
87	  ● api: Compiles               ✓
88	  ● api: Tests pass              ✓
89	  ● api: Health endpoint         ✓
90	    api: 3 passed  (2.1s)
91	
92	  ● worker: Builds              ✓
93	  ● worker: Connects to Redis    ✓
94	    worker: 2 passed  (1.8s)
95	
96	  ● auth: Builds                ✓
97	  ● auth: JWT validation         ✓
98	    auth: 2 passed  (1.2s)
99	
100	  Total: 3 projects, 7 passed  (5.1s)
101	```
102	
103	### Testing
104	
105	| Test | Description |
106	|------|-------------|
107	| TestDiscover_FindsSubConfigs | Create temp dir with 2 subdirs each having `.smoke.yaml`, verify both found |
108	| TestDiscover_SkipsIgnoredDirs | `node_modules/` with `.smoke.yaml` is skipped |
109	| TestDiscover_CustomExclude | `monorepo_exclude` dirs are skipped |
110	| TestDiscover_DeepNesting | Finds config 3+ levels deep |
111	| TestRunMonorepo_AllPass | Run monorepo suite, all pass |
112	| TestRunMonorepo_PartialFail | One sub-suite fails, aggregate exit code is 1 |
113	| TestRunMonorepo_TagFilter | `--tag build` runs only tagged tests across all sub-suites |
114	| TestRunMonorepo_DryRun | Lists discovered configs without running |
115	
116	## Feature 2: WebSocket Assertion
117	
118	### Schema
119	
120	```yaml
121	expect:
122	  websocket:
123	    url: "ws://localhost:8080/ws"      # ws:// or wss:// (required)
124	    send: '{"type": "ping"}'           # optional message to send after connect
125	    expect_contains: "pong"            # substring match on received message
126	    expect_matches: "connected.*true"  # regex match (alternative to contains)
127	    timeout: 5s                        # connection + response timeout
128	```
129	
130	New struct:
131	```go
132	type WebSocketCheck struct {
133	    URL            string   `yaml:"url"`
134	    Send           string   `yaml:"send,omitempty"`
135	    ExpectContains string   `yaml:"expect_contains,omitempty"`
136	    ExpectMatches  string   `yaml:"expect_matches,omitempty"`
137	    Timeout        Duration `yaml:"timeout,omitempty"`
138	}
139	```
140	
141	New field on `Expect`:
142	```go
143	WebSocket *WebSocketCheck `yaml:"websocket,omitempty"`
144	```
145	
146	### Validation
147	
148	- `url` is required, must start with `ws://` or `wss://`
149	- At least one of `expect_contains` or `expect_matches` is required
150	- If `expect_matches` is set, must compile as valid Go regex
151	
152	### Implementation
153	
154	Pure stdlib WebSocket client (~80 lines):
155	- HTTP GET with `Upgrade: websocket`, `Connection: Upgrade` headers
156	- `Sec-WebSocket-Key` via `crypto/rand` + `base64`
157	- `Sec-WebSocket-Accept` verification via `crypto/sha1`
158	- Frame parsing: opcode 1 (text), opcode 8 (close), opcode 9 (ping)
159	- Masking for client-to-server frames (required by RFC 6455)
160	
161	New `CheckWebSocket` function in `internal/runner/assertion.go` or new `assertion_ws.go`.
162	
163	Error handling:
164	- Connection refused → "connection failed: ..."
165	- Timeout → "timed out after Xs waiting for message"
166	- Close frame → "server closed connection: <reason>"
167	- No match → "received <message> did not contain/match <pattern>"
168	
169	### Testing
170	
171	| Test | Description |
172	|------|-------------|
173	| TestCheckWebSocket_ExpectContains_Pass | Server echoes, send "ping", expect "ping" |
174	| TestCheckWebSocket_ExpectMatches_Pass | Server sends JSON, regex matches |
175	| TestCheckWebSocket_NoMatch_Fail | Server sends "hello", expect "pong" |
176	| TestCheckWebSocket_ConnectionRefused | Connect to unused port, verify graceful failure |
177	
178	## Feature 3: Optional gRPC Module via Build Tag
179	
180	### Approach
181	
182	Opt-in: `-tags grpc`. Default build excludes gRPC deps entirely.
183	
184	### File Changes
185	
186	```
187	internal/runner/
188	  assertion_grpc.go          # //go:build grpc — current gRPC code, moved from assertion.go
189	  assertion_grpc_stub.go     # //go:build !grpc — stub returning "not available"
190	```
191	
192	`assertion_grpc.go` contains:
193	- `CheckGRPCHealth` function (moved from `assertion.go`)
194	- gRPC-related imports (`google.golang.org/grpc`, healthpb, etc.)
195	
196	`assertion_grpc_stub.go` contains:
197	```go
198	//go:build !grpc
199	
200	func CheckGRPCHealth(check *schema.GRPCHealthCheck) AssertionResult {
201	    return AssertionResult{
202	        Type:     "grpc_health",
203	        Expected: check.Address,
204	        Actual:   "grpc_health not available — rebuild with -tags grpc",
205	        Passed:   false,
206	    }
207	}
208	```
209	
210	### Impact
211	
212	| Build | Command | Binary Size | gRPC Available |
213	|-------|---------|-------------|----------------|
214	| Default | `go build -o smoke .` | ~8MB | No (stub returns error) |
215	| With gRPC | `go build -tags grpc -o smoke .` | ~13.9MB | Yes |
216	
217	- `go.mod` unchanged — build tags only control compilation
218	- Schema struct `GRPCHealthCheck` remains in schema.go (always parsed, validation always works)
219	- Runner wiring in `runner.go` unchanged — calls `CheckGRPCHealth` regardless
220	- No behavior change for users who install via `go install` (they get default build)
221	
222	### Testing
223	
224	Existing gRPC tests move to `assertion_grpc_test.go` with `//go:build grpc` tag. New stub test:
225	```go
226	//go:build !grpc
227	
228	func TestCheckGRPCHealth_StubReturns(t *testing.T) {
229	    result := CheckGRPCHealth(&schema.GRPCHealthCheck{Address: "localhost:9090"})
230	    if result.Passed {
231	        t.Error("stub should not pass")
232	    }
233	    if !strings.Contains(result.Actual, "grpc") {
234	        t.Error("should mention grpc in output")
235	    }
236	}
237	```
238	
239	## File Scope
240	
241	```yaml

---
title: "cosmo-smoke v0.7 — Monorepo, WebSocket, gRPC Build Tag"
created: 2026-04-18
status: approved
roadmap:
  - ROAD-010
  - ROAD-031
  - ROAD-030
---

# cosmo-smoke v0.7 — Monorepo, WebSocket, gRPC Build Tag

## Why

v0.6 expanded assertion coverage with 4 new connectivity checks. v0.7 addresses three gaps:

1. **Monorepo support (ROAD-010)** — CosmoLabs has ~95 projects. Many live in monorepos. Currently users must `cd` into each subdirectory or write a wrapper script. Auto-discovery makes `smoke run --monorepo` work from the repo root.

2. **WebSocket assertion (ROAD-031)** — Real-time apps use WebSocket extensively. A connect-send-expect assertion fills the gap between HTTP checks and custom shell scripts.

3. **Optional gRPC module (ROAD-030)** — The gRPC health check adds 121MB of dependencies and inflates the binary from ~8MB to 13.9MB. Most users don't need gRPC. An opt-in build tag lets them skip it.

## Design Decisions

1. **Monorepo is explicit opt-in.** `--monorepo` flag or `monorepo: true` in root config. No auto-detection — avoids accidental scans of large non-monorepo directories.
2. **Unlimited discovery depth.** Real monorepos have varied layouts (`services/team-a/api/`). Depth limits would be arbitrary.
3. **WebSocket via stdlib only.** The connect-send-expect pattern only needs HTTP upgrade + frame parsing. No external WS library. ~80 lines.
4. **gRPC is opt-in via `-tags grpc`.** Default build excludes gRPC. Users who need it add the tag. No behavior change for existing users.
5. **No new external dependencies.** All three features use stdlib only. The "minimal deps" principle is maintained.

## Feature 1: Monorepo Sub-Config Discovery

### Behavior

- `smoke run --monorepo` triggers discovery from cwd (or config file dir)
- `monorepo: true` in root `.smoke.yaml` settings also enables it
- CLI flag takes precedence over config
- Walks all subdirectories (unlimited depth), finds `.smoke.yaml` files
- Skips: `.git/`, `node_modules/`, `vendor/`, `__pycache__/`, `dist/`, `build/`, `target/`
- Additional skip dirs configurable via `monorepo_exclude` in settings
- Each discovered config runs as a sub-suite with its own project name
- Reporter shows per-project results + aggregate summary
- Exit code 1 if any sub-suite has failures
- `--tag`/`--exclude-tag`/`--fail-fast` apply across all sub-suites
- `--dry-run` lists all discovered configs without running
- Root `.smoke.yaml` is optional — can use `--monorepo` flag alone

### Schema Changes

```yaml
# Root .smoke.yaml (optional)
version: 1
project: my-monorepo
settings:
  monorepo: true
  monorepo_exclude:
    - "internal/"
    - "examples/"
```

New fields on `Settings`:
```go
Monorepo         bool     `yaml:"monorepo,omitempty"`
MonorepoExclude  []string `yaml:"monorepo_exclude,omitempty"`
```

### Implementation

New `internal/monorepo/` package:
- `Discover(root string, exclude []string) ([]SubConfig, error)` — walks directory tree
- `SubConfig` struct: `{Path, Project string}`
- Skips common non-project dirs by default

Runner changes:
- New `RunMonorepo(opts RunOptions) (*SuiteResult, error)` method
- Runs each sub-config as its own `Runner` instance
- Aggregates results into a single `SuiteResult`
- Reporter shows project-level grouping

CLI changes:
- `--monorepo` flag on `smoke run`
- `run.go`: check flag + config, call `RunMonorepo` when active

### Output Example

```
  ● api: Compiles               ✓
  ● api: Tests pass              ✓
  ● api: Health endpoint         ✓
    api: 3 passed  (2.1s)

  ● worker: Builds              ✓
  ● worker: Connects to Redis    ✓
    worker: 2 passed  (1.8s)

  ● auth: Builds                ✓
  ● auth: JWT validation         ✓
    auth: 2 passed  (1.2s)

  Total: 3 projects, 7 passed  (5.1s)
```

### Testing

| Test | Description |
|------|-------------|
| TestDiscover_FindsSubConfigs | Create temp dir with 2 subdirs each having `.smoke.yaml`, verify both found |
| TestDiscover_SkipsIgnoredDirs | `node_modules/` with `.smoke.yaml` is skipped |
| TestDiscover_CustomExclude | `monorepo_exclude` dirs are skipped |
| TestDiscover_DeepNesting | Finds config 3+ levels deep |
| TestRunMonorepo_AllPass | Run monorepo suite, all pass |
| TestRunMonorepo_PartialFail | One sub-suite fails, aggregate exit code is 1 |
| TestRunMonorepo_TagFilter | `--tag build` runs only tagged tests across all sub-suites |
| TestRunMonorepo_DryRun | Lists discovered configs without running |

## Feature 2: WebSocket Assertion

### Schema

```yaml
expect:
  websocket:
    url: "ws://localhost:8080/ws"      # ws:// or wss:// (required)
    send: '{"type": "ping"}'           # optional message to send after connect
    expect_contains: "pong"            # substring match on received message
    expect_matches: "connected.*true"  # regex match (alternative to contains)
    timeout: 5s                        # connection + response timeout
```

New struct:
```go
type WebSocketCheck struct {
    URL            string   `yaml:"url"`
    Send           string   `yaml:"send,omitempty"`
    ExpectContains string   `yaml:"expect_contains,omitempty"`
    ExpectMatches  string   `yaml:"expect_matches,omitempty"`
    Timeout        Duration `yaml:"timeout,omitempty"`
}
```

New field on `Expect`:
```go
WebSocket *WebSocketCheck `yaml:"websocket,omitempty"`
```

### Validation

- `url` is required, must start with `ws://` or `wss://`
- At least one of `expect_contains` or `expect_matches` is required
- If `expect_matches` is set, must compile as valid Go regex

### Implementation

Pure stdlib WebSocket client (~80 lines):
- HTTP GET with `Upgrade: websocket`, `Connection: Upgrade` headers
- `Sec-WebSocket-Key` via `crypto/rand` + `base64`
- `Sec-WebSocket-Accept` verification via `crypto/sha1`
- Frame parsing: opcode 1 (text), opcode 8 (close), opcode 9 (ping)
- Masking for client-to-server frames (required by RFC 6455)

New `CheckWebSocket` function in `internal/runner/assertion.go` or new `assertion_ws.go`.

Error handling:
- Connection refused → "connection failed: ..."
- Timeout → "timed out after Xs waiting for message"
- Close frame → "server closed connection: <reason>"
- No match → "received <message> did not contain/match <pattern>"

### Testing

| Test | Description |
|------|-------------|
| TestCheckWebSocket_ExpectContains_Pass | Server echoes, send "ping", expect "ping" |
| TestCheckWebSocket_ExpectMatches_Pass | Server sends JSON, regex matches |
| TestCheckWebSocket_NoMatch_Fail | Server sends "hello", expect "pong" |
| TestCheckWebSocket_ConnectionRefused | Connect to unused port, verify graceful failure |

## Feature 3: Optional gRPC Module via Build Tag

### Approach

Opt-in: `-tags grpc`. Default build excludes gRPC deps entirely.

### File Changes

```
internal/runner/
  assertion_grpc.go          # //go:build grpc — current gRPC code, moved from assertion.go
  assertion_grpc_stub.go     # //go:build !grpc — stub returning "not available"
```

`assertion_grpc.go` contains:
- `CheckGRPCHealth` function (moved from `assertion.go`)
- gRPC-related imports (`google.golang.org/grpc`, healthpb, etc.)

`assertion_grpc_stub.go` contains:
```go
//go:build !grpc

func CheckGRPCHealth(check *schema.GRPCHealthCheck) AssertionResult {
    return AssertionResult{
        Type:     "grpc_health",
        Expected: check.Address,
        Actual:   "grpc_health not available — rebuild with -tags grpc",
        Passed:   false,
    }
}
```

### Impact

| Build | Command | Binary Size | gRPC Available |
|-------|---------|-------------|----------------|
| Default | `go build -o smoke .` | ~8MB | No (stub returns error) |
| With gRPC | `go build -tags grpc -o smoke .` | ~13.9MB | Yes |

- `go.mod` unchanged — build tags only control compilation
- Schema struct `GRPCHealthCheck` remains in schema.go (always parsed, validation always works)
- Runner wiring in `runner.go` unchanged — calls `CheckGRPCHealth` regardless
- No behavior change for users who install via `go install` (they get default build)

### Testing

Existing gRPC tests move to `assertion_grpc_test.go` with `//go:build grpc` tag. New stub test:
```go
//go:build !grpc

func TestCheckGRPCHealth_StubReturns(t *testing.T) {
    result := CheckGRPCHealth(&schema.GRPCHealthCheck{Address: "localhost:9090"})
    if result.Passed {
        t.Error("stub should not pass")
    }
    if !strings.Contains(result.Actual, "grpc") {
        t.Error("should mention grpc in output")
    }
}
```

## File Scope

```yaml
files_modified:
  - cmd/run.go                      # --monorepo flag
  - internal/schema/schema.go       # new Settings fields, WebSocketCheck struct
  - internal/schema/validate.go     # WebSocket + monorepo validation
  - internal/runner/runner.go       # RunMonorepo method, WebSocket wiring
  - internal/runner/assertion.go    # Remove gRPC code, add CheckWebSocket
  - CLAUDE.md                       # Update assertion table
files_created:
  - internal/monorepo/discover.go   # SubConfig discovery
  - internal/monorepo/discover_test.go
  - internal/runner/assertion_grpc.go       # +build grpc
  - internal/runner/assertion_grpc_stub.go  # +build !grpc
  - internal/runner/assertion_ws_test.go    # WebSocket tests
  - internal/runner/assertion_grpc_test.go  # gRPC tests (moved, +build grpc)
```

## v0.7 Release Scope

Headline: "cosmo-smoke v0.7 — Monorepo, WebSocket, Lean Binary"

- Monorepo sub-config auto-discovery (ROAD-010)
- WebSocket connect-send-expect assertion (ROAD-031)
- Optional gRPC module via build tag (ROAD-030)
- ~258+ tests passing (246 current + ~12 new)

## Design Decisions

1. **Monorepo is explicit opt-in.** `--monorepo` flag or `monorepo: true` in root config. No auto-detection — avoids accidental scans of large non-monorepo directories.
2. **Unlimited discovery depth.** Real monorepos have varied layouts (`services/team-a/api/`). Depth limits would be arbitrary.
3. **WebSocket via stdlib only.** The connect-send-expect pattern only needs HTTP upgrade + frame parsing. No external WS library. ~80 lines.
4. **gRPC is opt-in via `-tags grpc`.** Default build excludes gRPC. Users who need it add the tag. No behavior change for existing users.
5. **No new external dependencies.** All three features use stdlib only. The "minimal deps" principle is maintained.

## Feature 1: Monorepo Sub-Config Discovery

### Behavior

- `smoke run --monorepo` triggers discovery from cwd (or config file dir)
- `monorepo: true` in root `.smoke.yaml` settings also enables it
- CLI flag takes precedence over config
- Walks all subdirectories (unlimited depth), finds `.smoke.yaml` files
- Skips: `.git/`, `node_modules/`, `vendor/`, `__pycache__/`, `dist/`, `build/`, `target/`
- Additional skip dirs configurable via `monorepo_exclude` in settings
- Each discovered config runs as a sub-suite with its own project name
- Reporter shows per-project results + aggregate summary
- Exit code 1 if any sub-suite has failures
- `--tag`/`--exclude-tag`/`--fail-fast` apply across all sub-suites
- `--dry-run` lists all discovered configs without running
- `--watch` + `--monorepo`: watches all subdirectories for changes and re-runs the full suite. Re-discovers on each run (new configs are picked up).
- Root `.smoke.yaml` is optional — can use `--monorepo` flag alone
- If no `.smoke.yaml` files are found, returns error: "no smoke configs found in <dir>"

### Schema Changes

```yaml
# Root .smoke.yaml (optional)
version: 1
project: my-monorepo
settings:
  monorepo: true
  monorepo_exclude:
    - "internal/"
    - "examples/"
```

New fields on `Settings`:
```go
Monorepo         bool     `yaml:"monorepo,omitempty"`
MonorepoExclude  []string `yaml:"monorepo_exclude,omitempty"`
```

### Implementation

New `internal/monorepo/` package:
- `Discover(root string, exclude []string) ([]SubConfig, error)` — walks directory tree
- `SubConfig` struct: `{Path, Project string}`
- Skips common non-project dirs by default

Runner changes:
- New `RunMonorepo(opts RunOptions) (*SuiteResult, error)` method
- Runs each sub-config as its own `Runner` instance
- Aggregates results into a single `SuiteResult`
- Reporter shows project-level grouping

CLI changes:
- `--monorepo` flag on `smoke run`
- `run.go`: check flag + config, call `RunMonorepo` when active

### Output Example

```
  ● api: Compiles               ✓
  ● api: Tests pass              ✓
  ● api: Health endpoint         ✓
    api: 3 passed  (2.1s)

  ● worker: Builds              ✓
  ● worker: Connects to Redis    ✓
    worker: 2 passed  (1.8s)

  ● auth: Builds                ✓
  ● auth: JWT validation         ✓
    auth: 2 passed  (1.2s)

  Total: 3 projects, 7 passed  (5.1s)
```

### Testing

| Test | Description |
|------|-------------|
| TestDiscover_FindsSubConfigs | Create temp dir with 2 subdirs each having `.smoke.yaml`, verify both found |
| TestDiscover_SkipsIgnoredDirs | `node_modules/` with `.smoke.yaml` is skipped |
| TestDiscover_CustomExclude | `monorepo_exclude` dirs are skipped |
| TestDiscover_DeepNesting | Finds config 3+ levels deep |
| TestRunMonorepo_AllPass | Run monorepo suite, all pass |
| TestRunMonorepo_PartialFail | One sub-suite fails, aggregate exit code is 1 |
| TestRunMonorepo_TagFilter | `--tag build` runs only tagged tests across all sub-suites |
| TestRunMonorepo_DryRun | Lists discovered configs without running |

## Feature 2: WebSocket Assertion

### Schema

```yaml
expect:
  websocket:
    url: "ws://localhost:8080/ws"      # ws:// or wss:// (required)
    send: '{"type": "ping"}'           # optional message to send after connect
    expect_contains: "pong"            # substring match on received message
    expect_matches: "connected.*true"  # regex match (alternative to contains)
    timeout: 5s                        # connection + response timeout
```

New struct:
```go
type WebSocketCheck struct {
    URL            string   `yaml:"url"`
    Send           string   `yaml:"send,omitempty"`
    ExpectContains string   `yaml:"expect_contains,omitempty"`
    ExpectMatches  string   `yaml:"expect_matches,omitempty"`
    Timeout        Duration `yaml:"timeout,omitempty"`
}
```

New field on `Expect`:
```go
WebSocket *WebSocketCheck `yaml:"websocket,omitempty"`
```

### Validation

- `url` is required, must start with `ws://` or `wss://`
- At least one of `expect_contains` or `expect_matches` is required
- If `expect_matches` is set, must compile as valid Go regex

### Implementation

Pure stdlib WebSocket client (~80 lines):
- HTTP GET with `Upgrade: websocket`, `Connection: Upgrade` headers
- `Sec-WebSocket-Key` via `crypto/rand` + `base64`
- `Sec-WebSocket-Accept` verification via `crypto/sha1`
- Frame parsing: opcode 1 (text), opcode 8 (close), opcode 9 (ping)
- Masking for client-to-server frames (required by RFC 6455)

New `CheckWebSocket` function in `internal/runner/assertion.go` or new `assertion_ws.go`.

Error handling:
- Connection refused → "connection failed: ..."
- Timeout → "timed out after Xs waiting for message"
- Close frame → "server closed connection: <reason>"
- No match → "received <message> did not contain/match <pattern>"

### Testing

| Test | Description |
|------|-------------|
| TestCheckWebSocket_ExpectContains_Pass | Server echoes, send "ping", expect "ping" |
| TestCheckWebSocket_ExpectMatches_Pass | Server sends JSON, regex matches |
| TestCheckWebSocket_NoMatch_Fail | Server sends "hello", expect "pong" |
| TestCheckWebSocket_ConnectionRefused | Connect to unused port, verify graceful failure |

## Feature 3: Optional gRPC Module via Build Tag

### Approach

Opt-in: `-tags grpc`. Default build excludes gRPC deps entirely.

### File Changes

```
internal/runner/
  assertion_grpc.go          # //go:build grpc — current gRPC code, moved from assertion.go
  assertion_grpc_stub.go     # //go:build !grpc — stub returning "not available"
```

`assertion_grpc.go` contains:
- `CheckGRPCHealth` function (moved from `assertion.go`)
- gRPC-related imports (`google.golang.org/grpc`, healthpb, etc.)

`assertion_grpc_stub.go` contains:
```go
//go:build !grpc

func CheckGRPCHealth(check *schema.GRPCHealthCheck) AssertionResult {
    return AssertionResult{
        Type:     "grpc_health",
        Expected: check.Address,
        Actual:   "grpc_health not available — rebuild with -tags grpc",
        Passed:   false,
    }
}
```

### Impact

| Build | Command | Binary Size | gRPC Available |
|-------|---------|-------------|----------------|
| Default | `go build -o smoke .` | ~8MB | No (stub returns error) |
| With gRPC | `go build -tags grpc -o smoke .` | ~13.9MB | Yes |

- `go.mod` unchanged — build tags only control compilation
- Schema struct `GRPCHealthCheck` remains in schema.go (always parsed, validation always works)
- Runner wiring in `runner.go` unchanged — calls `CheckGRPCHealth` regardless
- No behavior change for users who install via `go install` (they get default build)

### Testing

Existing gRPC tests move to `assertion_grpc_test.go` with `//go:build grpc` tag. New stub test:
```go
//go:build !grpc

func TestCheckGRPCHealth_StubReturns(t *testing.T) {
    result := CheckGRPCHealth(&schema.GRPCHealthCheck{Address: "localhost:9090"})
    if result.Passed {
        t.Error("stub should not pass")
    }
    if !strings.Contains(result.Actual, "grpc") {
        t.Error("should mention grpc in output")
    }
}
```

## File Scope

```yaml
files_modified:
  - cmd/run.go                      # --monorepo flag
  - internal/schema/schema.go       # new Settings fields, WebSocketCheck struct
  - internal/schema/validate.go     # WebSocket + monorepo validation
  - internal/runner/runner.go       # RunMonorepo method, WebSocket wiring
  - internal/runner/assertion.go    # Remove gRPC code, add CheckWebSocket
  - CLAUDE.md                       # Update assertion table
files_created:
  - internal/monorepo/discover.go   # SubConfig discovery
  - internal/monorepo/discover_test.go
  - internal/runner/assertion_grpc.go       # +build grpc
  - internal/runner/assertion_grpc_stub.go  # +build !grpc
  - internal/runner/assertion_ws_test.go    # WebSocket tests
  - internal/runner/assertion_grpc_test.go  # gRPC tests (moved, +build grpc)
```

## v0.7 Release Scope

Headline: "cosmo-smoke v0.7 — Monorepo, WebSocket, Lean Binary"

- Monorepo sub-config auto-discovery (ROAD-010)
- WebSocket connect-send-expect assertion (ROAD-031)
- Optional gRPC module via build tag (ROAD-030)
- ~258+ tests passing (246 current + ~12 new)

lines: +| TestDiscover_NoSmokeFiles | Directory tree with zero `.smoke.yaml` returns empty slice, runner returns error |
| TestRunMonorepo_AllPass | Run monorepo suite, all pass |
| TestRunMonorepo_PartialFail | One sub-suite fails, aggregate exit code is 1 |
| TestRunMonorepo_TagFilter | `--tag build` runs only tagged tests across all sub-suites |

input.new_string:
### Schema

```yaml
expect:
  websocket:
    url: "ws://localhost:8080/ws"      # ws:// or wss:// (required)
    send: '{"type": "ping"}'           # optional message to send after connect
    expect_contains: "pong"            # substring match on received message
    expect_matches: "connected.*true"  # regex match (alternative to contains)
    timeout: 5s                        # connection + response timeout (default 10s)
```

Two flows are supported:

1. **Connect-send-expect** (both `send` and `expect_*` set): Connect → send message → wait for response → match.
2. **Connect-and-wait** (`send` empty, `expect_*` set): Connect → wait for server to send an unsolicited message → match.
3. **Connect-only** (neither `send` nor `expect_*` set): Just verify the WebSocket handshake succeeds. Passes if upgrade response is 101.

New struct:
```go
type WebSocketCheck struct {
    URL            string   `yaml:"url"`
    Send           string   `yaml:"send,omitempty"`
    ExpectContains string   `yaml:"expect_contains,omitempty"`
    ExpectMatches  string   `yaml:"expect_matches,omitempty"`
    Timeout        Duration `yaml:"timeout,omitempty"` // default 10s
}
```

New field on `Expect`:
```go
WebSocket *WebSocketCheck `yaml:"websocket,omitempty"`
```

### Validation

- `url` is required, must start with `ws://` or `wss://`
- If `expect_matches` is set, must compile as valid Go regex
- It is valid to omit both `expect_contains` and `expect_matches` (connect-only mode)

### Implementation

Pure stdlib WebSocket client (~100 lines):
- HTTP GET with `Upgrade: websocket`, `Connection: Upgrade` headers
- `Sec-WebSocket-Key` via `crypto/rand` + `base64`
- `Sec-WebSocket-Accept` verification via `crypto/sha1`
- Frame parsing: opcode 1 (text), opcode 2 (binary — read payload but match as text), opcode 8 (close), opcode 9 (ping/pong)
- Masking for client-to-server frames (required by RFC 6455)
- Default timeout: 10s (consistent with HTTPCheck default)

New `CheckWebSocket` function in `internal/runner/assertion_ws.go`.

Error handling:
- Connection refused → "connection failed: ..."
- Timeout → "timed out after Xs waiting for message"
- Close frame → "server closed connection: <reason>"
- No match → "received <message> did not contain/match <pattern>"

### Testing

Test server: `httptest.NewServer` with a custom handler that performs the WebSocket upgrade handshake using the same stdlib frame logic (upgrade handler in test helper file, ~40 lines).

| Test | Description |
|------|-------------|
| TestCheckWebSocket_ExpectContains_Pass | Server echoes, send "ping", expect "ping" |
| TestCheckWebSocket_ExpectMatches_Pass | Server sends JSON, regex matches |
| TestCheckWebSocket_NoMatch_Fail | Server sends "hello", expect "pong" |
| TestCheckWebSocket_ConnectionRefused | Connect to unused port, verify graceful failure |
| TestCheckWebSocket_ConnectOnly | No send/expect, verify handshake success |
| TestCheckWebSocket_BinaryFrame | Server sends binary frame, assertion reads it as text |

# cosmo-smoke v0.7 — Monorepo, WebSocket, gRPC Build Tag

## Why

v0.6 expanded assertion coverage with 4 new connectivity checks. v0.7 addresses three gaps:

1. **Monorepo support (ROAD-010)** — CosmoLabs has ~95 projects. Many live in monorepos. Currently users must `cd` into each subdirectory or write a wrapper script. Auto-discovery makes `smoke run --monorepo` work from the repo root.

2. **WebSocket assertion (ROAD-031)** — Real-time apps use WebSocket extensively. A connect-send-expect assertion fills the gap between HTTP checks and custom shell scripts.

3. **Optional gRPC module (ROAD-030)** — The gRPC health check adds 121MB of dependencies and inflates the binary from ~8MB to 13.9MB. Most users don't need gRPC. An opt-in build tag lets them skip it.

## Design Decisions

1. **Monorepo is explicit opt-in.** `--monorepo` flag or `monorepo: true` in root config. No auto-detection — avoids accidental scans of large non-monorepo directories.
2. **Unlimited discovery depth.** Real monorepos have varied layouts (`services/team-a/api/`). Depth limits would be arbitrary.
3. **WebSocket via stdlib only.** The connect-send-expect pattern only needs HTTP upgrade + frame parsing. No external WS library. ~80 lines.
4. **gRPC is opt-in via `-tags grpc`.** Default build excludes gRPC. Users who need it add the tag. No behavior change for existing users.
5. **No new external dependencies.** All three features use stdlib only. The "minimal deps" principle is maintained.

## Feature 1: Monorepo Sub-Config Discovery

### Behavior

- `smoke run --monorepo` triggers discovery from cwd (or config file dir)
- `monorepo: true` in root `.smoke.yaml` settings also enables it
- CLI flag takes precedence over config
- Walks all subdirectories (unlimited depth), finds `.smoke.yaml` files
- Skips: `.git/`, `node_modules/`, `vendor/`, `__pycache__/`, `dist/`, `build/`, `target/`
- Additional skip dirs configurable via `monorepo_exclude` in settings
- Each discovered config runs as a sub-suite with its own project name
- Reporter shows per-project results + aggregate summary
- Exit code 1 if any sub-suite has failures
- `--tag`/`--exclude-tag`/`--fail-fast` apply across all sub-suites
- `--dry-run` lists all discovered configs without running
- `--watch` + `--monorepo`: watches all subdirectories for changes and re-runs the full suite. Re-discovers on each run (new configs are picked up).
- Root `.smoke.yaml` is optional — can use `--monorepo` flag alone
- If no `.smoke.yaml` files are found, returns error: "no smoke configs found in <dir>"

### Schema Changes

```yaml

version: 1
project: my-monorepo
settings:
  monorepo: true
  monorepo_exclude:
    - "internal/"
    - "examples/"
```

New fields on `Settings`:
```go
Monorepo         bool     `yaml:"monorepo,omitempty"`
MonorepoExclude  []string `yaml:"monorepo_exclude,omitempty"`
```

- Skips common non-project dirs by default

- Aggregates results into a single `SuiteResult`
- Reporter shows project-level grouping

CLI changes:
- `--monorepo` flag on `smoke run`
- `run.go`: check flag + config, call `RunMonorepo` when active

```
  ● api: Compiles               ✓
  ● api: Tests pass              ✓
  ● api: Health endpoint         ✓
    api: 3 passed  (2.1s)

  ● worker: Builds              ✓
  ● worker: Connects to Redis    ✓
    worker: 2 passed  (1.8s)

  ● auth: Builds                ✓
  ● auth: JWT validation         ✓
    auth: 2 passed  (1.2s)
```

| Test | Description |
|------|-------------|
| TestCheckWebSocket_ExpectContains_Pass | Server echoes, send "ping", expect "ping" |
| TestCheckWebSocket_ExpectMatches_Pass | Server sends JSON, regex matches |
| TestCheckWebSocket_NoMatch_Fail | Server sends "hello", expect "pong" |
| TestCheckWebSocket_ConnectionRefused | Connect to unused port, verify graceful failure |

## Feature 3: Optional gRPC Module via Build Tag

### Approach

Opt-in: `-tags grpc`. Default build excludes gRPC deps entirely.

### File Changes

```
internal/runner/
  assertion_grpc.go          # //go:build grpc — current gRPC code, moved from assertion.go
  assertion_grpc_stub.go     # //go:build !grpc — stub returning "not available"
```

`assertion_grpc.go` contains:
- `CheckGRPCHealth` function (moved from `assertion.go`)
- gRPC-related imports (`google.golang.org/grpc`, healthpb, etc.)

`assertion_grpc_stub.go` contains:
```go
//go:build !grpc

func CheckGRPCHealth(check *schema.GRPCHealthCheck) AssertionResult {
    return AssertionResult{
        Type:     "grpc_health",
        Expected: check.Address,
        Actual:   "grpc_health not available — rebuild with -tags grpc",
        Passed:   false,
    }
}
```

### Impact

| Build | Command | Binary Size | gRPC Available |
|-------|---------|-------------|----------------|
| Default | `go build -o smoke .` | ~8MB | No (stub returns error) |
| With gRPC | `go build -tags grpc -o smoke .` | ~13.9MB | Yes |

- `go.mod` unchanged — build tags only control compilation
- Schema struct `GRPCHealthCheck` remains in schema.go (always parsed, validation always works)
- Runner wiring in `runner.go` unchanged — calls `CheckGRPCHealth` regardless
- No behavior change for users who install via `go install` (they get default build)

### Testing

Existing gRPC tests move to `assertion_grpc_test.go` with `//go:build grpc` tag. New stub test:
```go
//go:build !grpc

func TestCheckGRPCHealth_StubReturns(t *testing.T) {
    result := CheckGRPCHealth(&schema.GRPCHealthCheck{Address: "localhost:9090"})
    if result.Passed {
        t.Error("stub should not pass")
    }
    if !strings.Contains(result.Actual, "grpc") {
        t.Error("should mention grpc in output")
    }
}
```

## File Scope

```yaml
files_modified:
  - cmd/run.go                      # --monorepo flag
  - internal/schema/schema.go       # new Settings fields, WebSocketCheck struct
  - internal/schema/validate.go     # WebSocket + monorepo validation
  - internal/runner/runner.go       # RunMonorepo method, WebSocket wiring
  - internal/runner/assertion.go    # Remove gRPC code, add CheckWebSocket
  - CLAUDE.md                       # Update assertion table
files_created:
  - internal/monorepo/discover.go   # SubConfig discovery
  - internal/monorepo/discover_test.go
  - internal/runner/assertion_grpc.go       # +build grpc
  - internal/runner/assertion_grpc_stub.go  # +build !grpc
  - internal/runner/assertion_ws_test.go    # WebSocket tests
  - internal/runner/assertion_grpc_test.go  # gRPC tests (moved, +build grpc)
```

## v0.7 Release Scope

Headline: "cosmo-smoke v0.7 — Monorepo, WebSocket, Lean Binary"

- Monorepo sub-config auto-discovery (ROAD-010)
- WebSocket connect-send-expect assertion (ROAD-031)
- Optional gRPC module via build tag (ROAD-030)
- ~258+ tests passing (246 current + ~12 new)

lines: send: '{"type": "ping"}'           # optional message to send after connect
expect_contains: "pong"            # substring match on received message
+1. **Connect-send-expect** (both `send` and `expect_*` set): Connect → send message → wait for response → match.
+2. **Connect-and-wait** (`send` empty, `expect_*` set): Connect → wait for server to send an unsolicited message → match.
+3. **Connect-only** (neither `send` nor `expect_*` set): Just verify the WebSocket handshake succeeds. Passes if upgrade response is 101.
- `url` is required, must start with `ws://` or `wss://`
-- At least one of `expect_contains` or `expect_matches` is required
- If `expect_matches` is set, must compile as valid Go regex
+- It is valid to omit both `expect_contains` and `expect_matches` (connect-only mode)
- HTTP GET with `Upgrade: websocket`, `Connection: Upgrade` headers
+- Frame parsing: opcode 1 (text), opcode 2 (binary — read payload but match as text), opcode 8 (close), opcode 9 (ping/pong)
- Masking for client-to-server frames (required by RFC 6455)
+Test server: `httptest.NewServer` with a custom handler that performs the WebSocket upgrade handshake using the same stdlib frame logic (upgrade handler in test helper file, ~40 lines).
| TestCheckWebSocket_ExpectMatches_Pass | Server sends JSON, regex matches |
| TestCheckWebSocket_ConnectionRefused | Connect to unused port, verify graceful failure |
+| TestCheckWebSocket_ConnectOnly | No send/expect, verify handshake success |
+| TestCheckWebSocket_BinaryFrame | Server sends binary frame, assertion reads it as text |
+| TestCheckWebSocket_ExpectMatches_Pass | Server sends JSON, regex matches |
+| TestCheckWebSocket_ConnectionRefused | Connect to unused port, verify graceful failure |
## Feature 3: Optional gRPC Module via Build Tag

content.text:
Base directory for this skill: /Users/gab/.claude/plugins/cache/superpowers-marketplace/superpowers/5.0.2/skills/writing-plans

# Writing Plans

## Overview

Write comprehensive implementation plans assuming the engineer has zero context for our codebase and questionable taste. Document everything they need to know: which files to touch for each task, code, testing, docs they might need to check, how to test it. Give them the whole plan as bite-sized tasks. DRY. YAGNI. TDD. Frequent commits.

Assume they are a skilled developer, but know almost nothing about our toolset or problem domain. Assume they don't know good test design very well.

**Announce at start:** "I'm using the writing-plans skill to create the implementation plan."

**Context:** This should be run in a dedicated worktree (created by brainstorming skill).

**Save plans to:** `docs/superpowers/plans/YYYY-MM-DD-<feature-name>.md`
- (User preferences for plan location override this default)

## Scope Check

If the spec covers multiple independent subsystems, it should have been broken into sub-project specs during brainstorming. If it wasn't, suggest breaking this into separate plans — one per subsystem. Each plan should produce working, testable software on its own.

## File Structure

Before defining tasks, map out which files will be created or modified and what each one is responsible for. This is where decomposition decisions get locked in.

- Design units with clear boundaries and well-defined interfaces. Each file should have one clear responsibility.
- You reason best about code you can hold in context at once, and your edits are more reliable when files are focused. Prefer smaller, focused files over large ones that do too much.
- Files that change together should live together. Split by responsibility, not by technical layer.
- In existing codebases, follow established patterns. If the codebase uses large files, don't unilaterally restructure - but if a file you're modifying has grown unwieldy, including a split in the plan is reasonable.

This structure informs the task decomposition. Each task should produce self-contained changes that make sense independently.

## Bite-Sized Task Granularity

**Each step is one action (2-5 minutes):**
- "Write the failing test" - step
- "Run it to make sure it fails" - step
- "Implement the minimal code to make the test pass" - step
- "Run the tests and make sure they pass" - step
- "Commit" - step

## Plan Document Header

**Every plan MUST start with this header:**

```markdown
# [Feature Name] Implementation Plan

> **For agentic workers:** REQUIRED: Use superpowers:subagent-driven-development (if subagents available) or superpowers:executing-plans to implement this plan. Steps use checkbox (`- [ ]`) syntax for tracking.

**Goal:** [One sentence describing what this builds]

**Architecture:** [2-3 sentences about approach]

**Tech Stack:** [Key technologies/libraries]

---
```

## Task Structure

````markdown
### Task N: [Component Name]

**Files:**
- Create: `exact/path/to/file.py`
- Modify: `exact/path/to/existing.py:123-145`
- Test: `tests/exact/path/to/test.py`

- [ ] **Step 1: Write the failing test**

```python
def test_specific_behavior():
    result = function(input)
    assert result == expected
```

- [ ] **Step 2: Run test to verify it fails**

Run: `pytest tests/path/test.py::test_name -v`
Expected: FAIL with "function not defined"

- [ ] **Step 3: Write minimal implementation**

```python
def function(input):
    return expected
```

- [ ] **Step 4: Run test to verify it passes**

Run: `pytest tests/path/test.py::test_name -v`
Expected: PASS

- [ ] **Step 5: Commit**

```bash
git add tests/path/test.py src/path/file.py
git commit -m "feat: add specific feature"
```
````

## Remember
- Exact file paths always
- Complete code in plan (not "add validation")
- Exact commands with expected output
- Reference relevant skills with @ syntax
- DRY, YAGNI, TDD, frequent commits

## Plan Review Loop

After completing each chunk of the plan:

1. Dispatch plan-document-reviewer subagent (see plan-document-reviewer-prompt.md) with precisely crafted review context — never your session history. This keeps the reviewer focused on the plan, not your thought process.
   - Provide: chunk content, path to spec document
2. If ❌ Issues Found:
   - Fix the issues in the chunk
   - Re-dispatch reviewer for that chunk
   - Repeat until ✅ Approved
3. If ✅ Approved: proceed to next chunk (or execution handoff if last chunk)

**Chunk boundaries:** Use `## Chunk N: <name>` headings to delimit chunks. Each chunk should be ≤1000 lines and logically self-contained.

**Review loop guidance:**
- Same agent that wrote the plan fixes it (preserves context)
- If loop exceeds 5 iterations, surface to human for guidance
- Reviewers are advisory - explain disagreements if you believe feedback is incorrect

## Execution Handoff

After saving the plan:

**"Plan complete and saved to `docs/superpowers/plans/<filename>.md`. Ready to execute?"**

**Execution path depends on harness capabilities:**

**If harness has subagents (Claude Code, etc.):**
- **REQUIRED:** Use superpowers:subagent-driven-development
- Do NOT offer a choice - subagent-driven is the standard approach
- Fresh subagent per task + two-stage review

**If harness does NOT have subagents:**
- Execute plan in current session using superpowers:executing-plans
- Batch execution with checkpoints for review

I'm using the writing-plans skill to create the implementation plan.

Let me first map out the file structure, then write the plan.

# cosmo-smoke v0.7 — Monorepo, WebSocket, gRPC Build Tag

## Context

v0.6 shipped 4 new assertion types (url_reachable, service_reachable, s3_bucket, version_check) plus pre-commit hook integration. v0.7 adds monorepo sub-config discovery (ROAD-010), WebSocket assertion (ROAD-031), and optional gRPC build tag (ROAD-030). Three independent features, no new external deps.

Design spec: `docs/brainstorming/2026-04-18-v0-7-monorepo-websocket-grpc-buildtag.md`
Implementation plan: `docs/planning-mode/2026-04-18-v0-7-monorepo-websocket-grpc-buildtag.md`

## Goals

### [ ] 1. Add WebSocket schema struct + validation

Add WebSocketCheck struct to schema.go, wire into Expect, add validation rules for url format and regex.

### [ ] 2. Implement WebSocket client + Check function

Create assertion_ws.go with stdlib-only WebSocket client (upgrade, frame parsing, send). Add CheckWebSocket function. Wire into runner.go.

### [ ] 3. Write WebSocket tests

Create assertion_ws_test.go with httptest-based test server. 5 tests: expect_contains pass, expect_matches pass, no match fail, connection refused, connect-only.

### [ ] 4. Split gRPC code into build-tagged files

Move CheckGRPCHealth + gRPC imports to assertion_grpc.go (`//go:build grpc`). Create assertion_grpc_stub.go (`//go:build !grpc`). Remove gRPC from assertion.go.

### [ ] 5. Move gRPC tests and add stub test

Move existing gRPC tests to assertion_grpc_test.go (`//go:build grpc`). Add stub test in assertion_grpc_stub_test.go (`//go:build !grpc`). Verify both build modes.

### [ ] 6. Create monorepo discovery package

Create internal/monorepo/discover.go with Discover() function. 5 tests: finds sub-configs, skips ignored dirs, custom exclude, deep nesting, no smoke files.

### [ ] 7. Add monorepo schema + CLI flag + runner integration

Add Settings.Monorepo + MonorepoExclude. Add --monorepo flag. Add RunMonorepo to runner. Wire in run.go.

### [ ] 8. Update docs + release v0.7.0

Update CLAUDE.md, README.md, USAGE.md, FEATURES.md. Run self-smoke. Bump version, tag.

## Execution Strategy

Chunk 1 (Tasks 1-3) and Chunk 2 (Tasks 4-5) are independent — can run in parallel via GLM agents in separate worktrees. Chunk 3 (Tasks 6-7) is independent of both. Chunk 4 (Task 8) depends on all prior tasks.

agents:
  - task: "WebSocket schema + assertion + tests"
    model: sonnet
    files: [internal/schema/schema.go, internal/schema/validate.go, internal/runner/assertion_ws.go, internal/runner/assertion_ws_test.go, internal/runner/runner.go]
    ready: true
  - task: "gRPC build tag split + tests"
    model: sonnet
    files: [internal/runner/assertion.go, internal/runner/assertion_grpc.go, internal/runner/assertion_grpc_stub.go, internal/runner/assertion_grpc_test.go, internal/runner/assertion_grpc_stub_test.go]
    ready: true
  - task: "Monorepo discovery + runner integration"
    model: sonnet
    files: [internal/monorepo/discover.go, internal/monorepo/discover_test.go, internal/schema/schema.go, internal/runner/runner.go, cmd/run.go]
    ready: true


Update CLAUDE.md, README.md, USAGE.md, FEATURES.md. Run self-smoke. Bump version, tag.

Chunk 1 (Tasks 1-3) and Chunk 2 (Tasks 4-5) are independent — can run in parallel via GLM agents in separate worktrees. Chunk 3 (Tasks 6-7) is independent of both. Chunk 4 (Task 8) depends on all prior tasks.
